itineraries.name: Reims grandes marques weekend
itineraries.theme: First-time Champagne: the great houses and chalk cellars of Reims

days.morning: Start in Reims with a cellar tour at Champagne Taittinger in the Saint-Nicaise crayeres, the Roman chalk pits beneath the city, followed by a Comtes de Champagne tasting.
days.lunch: Light lunch near the cathedral before an afternoon walk through the UNESCO coronation cathedral quarter.
days.afternoon: Afternoon cellar visit at Champagne Ruinart, the oldest Champagne house, to taste the Chardonnay-led Blanc de Blancs in its deep crayeres.
days.evening: Aperitif and oysters at Le Bocal near the Boulingrin, then a late grower-Champagne flight at Le Wine Bar by Le Vintage on the Place du Forum.
days.accommodation: Boutique hotel in central Reims near the cathedral.

days.morning: Morning visit at Champagne Veuve Clicquot to see the riddling tradition of Madame Clicquot and taste the Yellow Label and La Grande Dame.
days.lunch: Lunch at a Reims brasserie, then drive 30 minutes south to Epernay.
days.afternoon: Walk the Avenue de Champagne and tour Champagne Perrier-Jouet, finishing at Le Cellier with a Belle Epoque flight.
days.evening: Closing grower flight at C Comme Champagne on Rue Gambetta before dinner in Epernay.
days.accommodation: Return to Reims or stay central in Epernay.

itineraries.description: Two-day introduction to Champagne through the great houses of Reims and Epernay, from the Roman chalk crayeres to the Avenue de Champagne, with grower bars in the evenings.
itineraries.tip: Book big-house cellar tours two to three weeks ahead; Taittinger and Ruinart sell out fastest on weekends.

itineraries.name: Grower Champagne three-day tour
itineraries.theme: Recoltant-manipulant deep dive: Montagne de Reims and Cote des Blancs growers

days.morning: Drive into the Montagne de Reims for a morning appointment at Champagne Pierre Paillard in Grand Cru Bouzy to taste single-plot blanc de noirs.
days.lunch: Picnic lunch among the Grand Cru vines or a village bistro in Bouzy.
days.afternoon: Afternoon visit at Egly-Ouriet in Ambonnay for powerful, long-aged Pinot Noir Champagnes.
days.evening: Return to Epernay for a grower flight at C Comme Champagne, then dinner at La Cave a Champagne.

days.morning: Head to the Cote des Blancs for a morning at Champagne Larmandier-Bernier in Vertus to taste organic, low-dosage blanc de blancs.
days.lunch: Lunch in Vertus or Le Mesnil-sur-Oger between Chardonnay villages.
days.afternoon: Afternoon tasting at Champagne Agrapart or Pierre Peters in Le Mesnil-sur-Oger for Grand Cru Chardonnay.
days.evening: Dinner in Epernay and a nightcap of Ratafia at the Distillerie Jean Goyard boutique in nearby Ay if open, otherwise a late glass on the avenue.

days.morning: Drive south to the Cote des Bar for a morning with the cult grower Roses de Jeanne in Celles-sur-Ource, by allocation visit only.
days.lunch: Lunch in Bar-sur-Aube or among the Aube vineyards.
days.afternoon: Afternoon at biodynamic Vouette et Sorbee in Buxieres-sur-Arce on the Kimmeridgian slopes near the Chablis border.
days.evening: Return north to Epernay or continue toward Troyes for the night.

itineraries.description: Three days through Champagne's grower heartland, from Grand Cru Bouzy and Ambonnay to the Cote des Blancs and the Aube, tasting recoltant-manipulant terroir Champagnes.
itineraries.tip: Grower visits are appointment-only and some cult names are allocation-only; email weeks ahead and keep the schedule loose.

itineraries.name: Biodynamic Champagne long weekend
itineraries.theme: Green-farmed Champagne: certified organic and biodynamic growers

days.morning: Begin in Epernay at biodynamic Leclerc Briant, certified organic since 2004 and biodynamic since 2008, to taste the range up to the sea-aged Abyss.
days.lunch: Lunch in Epernay near the Avenue de Champagne.
days.afternoon: Drive to Grand Cru Ambonnay for an afternoon at Demeter-certified Champagne Marguet, tasting amphora-aged single-plot cuvees.
days.evening: Return to Epernay for a biodynamic-leaning grower flight at Le 25bis by Leclerc Briant on the avenue.
days.accommodation: Le 25bis guest house or a central Epernay hotel.

days.morning: Drive to Vertus in the Cote des Blancs for a morning at organic Champagne Larmandier-Bernier and its taut blanc de blancs.
days.lunch: Lunch in the Cote des Blancs before heading south to the Aube.
days.afternoon: Afternoon at Demeter-certified Vouette et Sorbee in Buxieres-sur-Arce, or biodynamic Marie-Courtin in Polisot, by appointment.
days.evening: Dinner in Troyes or back in Epernay with a glass of certified-organic grower Champagne.

itineraries.description: A two-day route through Champagne's certified organic and biodynamic growers, from Epernay and Grand Cru Ambonnay to the Cote des Blancs and the Aube.
itineraries.tip: Confirm certifications and visit policies when booking, because several biodynamic growers are tiny allocation-only estates that do not take walk-in visitors at the cellar door.
